title: Actinocephalus nodifer
taxonomy: Actinocephalus nodifer is classified within the family Eriocaulaceae and belongs to the order Poales. This classification places it among the monocotyledons, a group of flowering plants characterized by having one cotyledon in their seeds. At the rank of family, it is most closely related to other genera such as Eriocaulon and Syngonanthus. The order Poales is a large group of flowering plants that includes grasses and sedges, among others. Actinocephalus nodifer is native to South America, where it grows in a variety of habitats.

intro: Actinocephalus nodifer, also known as Brazilian feather grass, is a perennial grass native to the subtropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and infections, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Actinocephalus nodifer include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
